tweets.text:
Great rundown on VVV’s raise today. Kinda necessary. Much better than the usual “sell to VC’s first undisclosed allo and dump your head” model. 

Bullish imho. 

Especially bullish for the proper moonshot scenarios, where we are competing with tardfi giants, not crypto shitters.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
lot of people wanna understand if the venice raise is bullish or bearish for $vvv since it was an equity round.

my read: bullish structure, execution-dependent outcome.

the instruments are common. equity + token warrants are normal in crypto. what's uncommon is the sequencing.
tweets.full_text:
Great rundown on VVV’s raise today. Kinda necessary. Much better than the usual “sell to VC’s first undisclosed allo and dump your head” model.

Especially bullish for the proper moonshot scenarios, where we are competing with tardfi giants, not crypto shitters.

tweets.text:
They can suppress crude prices all they want, but at the end of the day, consumers use products. And in this case, gasoline prices have to go up to price OUT exports. If it doesn't, US won't have the storage buffers it needs.

And since the US will have to push prices up to price out exports, the rest of the world will need more gasoline, so unless China decides to lift export bans and start pumping out 2.5 million b/d of gasoline via exports, you just hit the wall.

The growing divergence between refining margins and crude prices means that, at this point, it doesn't matter what your product yield is; you just buy crude because you are going to make money. We are headed there.
tweets.full_text:
They can suppress crude prices all they want, but at the end of the day, consumers use products. And in this case, gasoline prices have to go up to price OUT exports. If it doesn't, US won't have the storage buffers it needs.

And since the US will have to push prices up to price out exports, the rest of the world will need more gasoline, so unless China decides to lift export bans and start pumping out 2.5 million b/d of gasoline via exports, you just hit the wall.

The growing divergence between refining margins and crude prices means that, at this point, it doesn't matter what your product yield is; you just buy crude because you are going to make money. We are headed there.

tweets.text:
Ethereum Institutional @ethereuminsti will play a central and key role as a neutral entity bringing institutional adoption to ethereum ethereum:native 

In their words…
“What’s been missing for Ethereum to date is a neutral counterpart on the other side of the table: a guide that convenes the full ecosystem around shared standards, connects institutions within the ecosystem to solutions / infrastructure providers and applications, and removes integration friction, from first pilot through scaled production.”

Bitmine @BitMNR is glad to support this effort $BMNR
